title: Complimenting a Meal
conversation: Lori and Jane compliment Lisa on the delicious dinner she prepared. At the end of the evening, Mike and Lori thank her. Jane: Lisa, this shrimp dish is out of this world! Lori: Yes, it's delicious. You really outdid yourself! Jane: You can always count on Lisa to serve a great meal. Lisa: Help yourselves to more. Kyle: I don't want to make a pig of myself. Lisa: It's going to go to waste if nobody eats it. Kyle: I'd hate to see it go to waste! I'll take a second helping. (for food) Jane: Kyle, save some room for dessert! Kyle: (patting large stomach) Don't worry, there's still plenty of room in here. (two hours later) Mike: We'd better hit the road. Thank you for a lovely time. Lori: Dinner was delicious. You and Todd really knocked yourselves out. It was a real treat. Lisa: It was our pleasure. Lori: We look forward to having you over soon.

idioms.meaning_vi: tuyệt vời, không gì sánh bằng
idioms.example: The dessert my mom made was out of this world.
idioms.context: Lisa, this shrimp dish is out of this world!

idioms.meaning_en: to do something better than you have ever done before
idioms.meaning_vi: vượt lên chính mình, làm tốt hơn cả mong đợi
idioms.example: This dinner is amazing, you really outdid yourself this time.

idioms.meaning_en: to rely or depend on someone
idioms.example: You can always count on him to be on time.
idioms.context: You can always count on Lisa to serve a great meal.

idioms.meaning_en: to serve oneself as much as one wants
idioms.meaning_vi: tự phục vụ, cứ tự nhiên
idioms.example: Please help yourselves to the snacks on the table.

idioms.meaning_en: to eat too much food greedily
idioms.meaning_vi: ăn quá nhiều, ăn như heo
idioms.example: I made a pig of myself at the buffet and now I feel sick.
idioms.context: I don't want to make a pig of myself.

idioms.meaning_en: to be discarded or not used effectively
idioms.example: Don't let that extra food go to waste; put it in the fridge.
idioms.context: It's going to go to waste if nobody eats it.

idioms.meaning_en: a second portion of the same food
idioms.meaning_vi: phần ăn thứ hai, lấy thêm đồ ăn
idioms.example: The pie was so good that I asked for a second helping.

idioms.meaning_en: to not eat too much so that you still have an appetite for later
idioms.meaning_vi: để dành bụng (để ăn món tiếp theo)
idioms.example: Make sure you save some room for the birthday cake.
idioms.context: Kyle, save some room for dessert!

idioms.example: It's getting late, so we should hit the road.

idioms.meaning_en: to put forth a great deal of effort
idioms.meaning_vi: vô cùng nỗ lực, làm việc vất vả để chiêu đãi
idioms.example: They really knocked themselves out to make the party a success.
idioms.context: You and Todd really knocked yourselves out.

idioms.meaning_en: a special and very enjoyable experience
idioms.meaning_vi: một sự đãi ngộ/điều tuyệt vời
idioms.example: Going to the opera was a real treat for her.

idioms.meaning_en: to invite someone to your house
idioms.meaning_vi: mời ai đó sang nhà chơi
idioms.example: We enjoyed having you over for dinner last night.
idioms.context: We look forward to having you over soon.
